zh.lifeSummary: Sir Edward De La Pomeroy是Thomas De la Pomeroy和Joan (Joanna) Chudleigh的兒子，於1378年出生在英國英格蘭可瓦爾特雷恭尼。他於1404年與Margaret Beville在英國英格蘭多瑟特比阿明斯特結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他於1441年被列為sheriff of devon。他去世於1446年5月3日在英國戴文貝里 波梅洛易享年68歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Sir Edward De La Pomeroy是Thomas De la Pomeroy和Joan (Joanna) Chudleigh的儿子，于1378年出生在英国英格兰可瓦爾特雷恭尼。他于1404年与Margaret Beville在英国英格兰多瑟特比阿明斯特结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他于1441年被列为sheriff of devon。他去世于1446年5月3日在英国戴文貝里 波梅洛易享年68岁。

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: La
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Cuong, Anh, Thanh, Ha, Hoang, Tuan, Chau, Hao, Hien, Hoa, Hue, Nam, Phong, Sinh, Tuong, Min, Tam, Tanh, Thai, Tham, Tinh, Yeong.  Chinese Hong, Hang, Sang, Tong, Chan, Chi, Hwa Ja, Hyung, Kam, Ki Bong, Kin, Man.
Vietnamese: from the Chinese surname 羅, see   Luo   1.
Vietnamese (Lã): from the Chinese surname 呂, see Lu 6 and 3 below.

variants.name: Luo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Jian, Yi, Ping, Yong, Zhen, Feng, Hong, Ming, Jin, Bin, Chen, Hai, Lan, Min, Huan, Hung, Shen, Tian.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 羅, meaning ‘net for catching birds’ or ‘to collect’ in Chinese: (i) from Luo (羅), the name of an ancient state (located in present-day Hubei province). After it was annexed by the state of Chu during the early Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), people from the state migrated southwards and adopted Luo (羅) as their surname. (ii) from the Po Duo Luo (破多羅) and Chi Luo (叱羅) families from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China, who changed their original surnames to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) from the personal name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅), leader of an ancient Turkic tribe who are believed to have migrated to China during the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ). His descendants inherited his name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅) as their surname and shortened it to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iv) from the name of one of the seven families from the Bandun Man, an ethnic group residing in ancient southwestern China (located mainly in present-day Sichuan province).

nameMeanings.name: Pomeroy
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin; mainly Devon and Dorset): habitational name from any of various places in northeastern France named with Old French pommeroie, pommeraie ‘apple orchard’ (collective of pomme ‘apple’). The name was established in Ireland in 1573.

variants.name: Pomroy
meanings: English: variant of   Pomeroy  .

nameMeanings.name: Edward
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

variants.name: Edoardo
meanings: From English. See Edward .

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: Sir Edward De La Pomeroy was born in 1378, in Tregony, Cornwall, England, United Kingdom as the son of Thomas De la Pomeroy and Joan (Joanna) Chudleigh. He married Margaret Beville in 1404, in Beaminster, Dorset, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 1 daughter. In 1441, at the age of 63, his occupation is listed as sheriff of devon. He died on 3 May 1446, in Berry Pomeroy, Devon, England, at the age of 68.

fr.lifeSummary: Sir Edward De La Pomeroy est né en 1378, à Tregony, Cornouailles, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, fils de Thomas De la Pomeroy et Joan (Joanna) Chudleigh. Il a épousé Margaret Beville en 1404, à Beaminster, Dorset, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 1 fille. En 1441, à l’âge de 63 ans, sa profession de son indiquée est sheriff of devon. Il est décédé le 3 mai 1446, à Berry Pomeroy, Devon, Angleterre, à l’âge de 68 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Sir Edward De La Pomeroy wurde 1378 in Tregony, Cornwall, England, United Kingdom, als Sohn von Thomas De la Pomeroy und Joan (Joanna) Chudleigh geboren. Er hat 1404 in Beaminster, Dorset, England, United Kingdom Margaret Beville geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 1 Tochter. 1441, als er 63 Jahre alt war, war sein Beruf als sheriff of devon aufgeführt. Er starb am 3. Mai 1446 im Alter von 68 Jahren in Berry Pomeroy, Devon, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Edward De La Pomeroyは1378年にイギリスイギリスコルンワルトレゴニーでThomas De la PomeroyとJoan (Joanna) Chudleighの息子として生まれた。1404年にイギリスイギリスドールセトビーミンスターでMargaret Bevilleと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。1441年，63歳の時，職業はsheriff of devonと記録されている。彼は1446年5月3日にイギリスデボンバーリー ポメロイで68歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Edward De La Pomeroy(은)는 1378년 영국 잉글랜드 코른월 트레곤이에서 Thomas De la Pomeroy(와)과 Joan (Joanna) Chudleigh의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 1404년 영국 잉글랜드 도르세트 브이민스터에서 Margaret Beville(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 1441년, 63세의 나이로 그의 직업은 sheriff of devon이었다. 그는 1446년 5월 3일 영국 데폰 베리 폼로이에서 68세의 나이로 사망했다.

Ele casou-se com Margaret Beville em 1404, em Beaminster, Dorset, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 1 filha. Em 1441, com 63 anos, sua profissão aparece como sheriff of devon. Ele faleceu em 3 de maio de 1446, em Berry Pomeroy, Devon, Inglaterra, com 68 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Sir Edward De La Pomeroy родился в 1378 г. в местности трегони, корнвалл, Англия, Великобритания. Его родителей звали Thomas De la Pomeroy и Joan (Joanna) Chudleigh. Он женился на женщине по имени Margaret Beville в 1404 г. в местности биминстир, дорсет, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 1 дочь. В 1441 году, в возрасте 63 лет,  он работал по профессии sheriff of devon. Он умер 3 мая 1446 г. в местности бирри помирои, девон, Англия в возрасте 68 лет.

Se casó con Margaret Beville en 1404, en Beaminster, Dorset, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 1 hija. En 1441, a la edad de 63 años, su ocupación registrada era sheriff of devon. Murió el 3 de mayo de 1446, en Berry Pomeroy, Devon, Inglaterra, a la edad de 68 años.
